task_completion.description: Create a Graphite dashboard named 'Peak Load Analysis'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Graphite dashboard interface is visible with the title 'Peak Load Analysis' at the top.

task_completion.description: Add the 'Hourly CPU Peaks' graph with the correct target
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph titled 'Hourly CPU Peaks' is present in the dashboard. In the graph's edit/target view, the target is exactly `summarize(servers.ec2_instance_1.cpu.utilization, "1hour", "max")`.

task_completion.description: Add the 'Worst Case Instance' graph with the correct target
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph titled 'Worst Case Instance' is present in the dashboard. In the graph's edit/target view, the target is exactly `highestMax(servers.ec2_instance_*.cpu.*, 1)`.

task_completion.description: Add the 'Fleet CPU Ceiling' graph with the correct target
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph titled 'Fleet CPU Ceiling' is present in the dashboard. In the graph's edit/target view, the target is exactly `maxSeries(servers.ec2_instance_1.cpu.utilization, servers.ec2_instance_2.cpu.utilization, servers.ec2_instance_3.cpu.cloudwatch_utilization)`.
A visual confirmation that the dashboard has been saved (e.g., a 'Dashboard saved' notification, or the dashboard is in a saved state without unsaved indicators).

integrity.description: Agent used the Graphite web interface to create the dashboard
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Graphite web UI (menus, dialogs, graph editors) rather than terminal commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Graphite application
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface shown is the genuine Graphite web application, not a text editor where the agent just typed out the answers.
The graphs in the dashboard show rendered lines or data points, indicating that the targets are successfully querying the underlying metric data.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected dashboard name is 'Peak Load Analysis'. The dashboard must contain exactly three graphs with the following titles and targets: 1) 'Hourly CPU Peaks' with target `summarize(servers.ec2_instance_1.cpu.utilization, "1hour", "max")`, 2) 'Worst Case Instance' with target `highestMax(servers.ec2_instance_*.cpu.*, 1)`, and 3) 'Fleet CPU Ceiling' with target `maxSeries(servers.ec2_instance_1.cpu.utilization, servers.ec2_instance_2.cpu.utilization, servers.ec2_instance_3.cpu.cloudwatch_utilization)`.
